2025-2026 [updated 2025.04.17]

678 ENME - Engineering, Mechanical

ENME464 Cost Analysis for Engineers (3 Credits)

An introduction to the financial and cost analysis aspects of product engineering. Introduces key elements of traditional engineering economics including interest, present worth, depreciation, taxes, inflation, financial statement analysis, and return on investment. Provides an introduction to cost modeling as it applies to product manufacturing and support. Cost modeling topics will include: manufacturing cost analysis, life-cycle cost modeling (reliability and warranty), and cost of ownership. Prerequisite: ENME392; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department.
